I grew up in a small town.

Hi David. I love hearing how people started. Where did you grow up?
I grew up in a small town in the south. My childhood was quiet but happy.
That sounds lovely. And what did you study?
I studied engineering. I graduated in 2008, and then I started my career.
Was your first job a big challenge?
Yes, it was. I knew almost no one, but I slowly overcame the difficulties.
Was there a turning point for you?
Definitely. I relocated to a new city, and that decision changed everything. I achieved my dream there.
I studied hard and started my career.
My first job was a real challenge.
I overcame every difficulty.
Relocating here is my proudest memory.

I still remember my first day at work, many years ago. I grew up in a small town, and I never imagined I would live in a big city. After I graduated, I moved to the capital and started my career. The first months were a real challenge. I knew almost no one, and I often felt alone. But little by little, I overcame my fears and made good friends. Looking back, that difficult year was an important turning point. It made me the person I am today.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah. I was thinking about our conversation yesterday, about big life decisions. Ten years ago, I made the biggest one of my life. I left my job and relocated to another country. Honestly, at first it was terrible. I missed my family, and everything felt strange. But I decided to stay and finish what I started. In the end, I achieved everything I wanted. It was the best decision I ever made. Call me later, I want to hear your story too. Bye!

When I was a child, I grew up in a small town. I studied hard and graduated from university. My first job was a challenge, but I overcame it. A few years later, I relocated to a new city. In the end, I achieved my dream.
